question: First identify the California hospital that satisfies both of these conditions: at least 250 TAVR hospital cases with an Average mortality rating in 2022, and at least 35,000 ambulatory surgery encounters in 2019. Then, for that hospital, find the county with the highest total of homeless inpatient encounters broken down by age. As of 2015, what was that county's world rank for gross domestic product per capita? Return the rank number only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import re
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/california-hospital-ratings-for-transcatheter-aortic-valve-replacement-tavr-procedures/files/2022-tavr-hospital-performance-ratings.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"


def normalize_text(value):
    value = str(value).replace("\n", " ").replace("\x96", "-").replace("\x97", "-").replace("–", "-").replace("—", "-").strip()
    value = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", value)
    return "" if value.lower() == "nan" else value


def normalize_name(value):
    value = normalize_text(value)
    return value.replace("Cedars - Sinai Medical Center", "Cedars-Sinai Medical Center")

obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)
df.columns = [normalize_text(column) for column in df.columns]
for column in df.columns:
    if df[column].dtype == object:
        df[column] = df[column].map(normalize_text)

df["Hospital"] = df["Hospital"].map(normalize_name)
df["Total Hospital TAVR Cases"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Total Hospital TAVR Cases"], errors="coerce")

mortality = df[df["Performance Measure"].eq("TAVR In-hospital/30-day Mortality")].copy()
answer = sorted(
    mortality.loc[
        mortality["Total Hospital TAVR Cases"].ge(250)
        & mortality["Performance Rating"].eq("Average"),
        "Hospital"
    ].tolist()
)
1.subquestion: Which California hospitals had at least 250 TAVR cases and an Average mortality rating in 2022?

2.fact:
import io
import re
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/effects-of-covid-19-on-hospital-utilization-trends-636d2/files/hospital-utilization-trends.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"


def normalize_text(value):
    value = str(value).replace("\n", " ").replace("\x96", "-").replace("\x97", "-").replace("–", "-").replace("—", "-").strip()
    value = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", value)
    return "" if value.lower() == "nan" else value

obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)
df.columns = [normalize_text(column) for column in df.columns]
for column in df.columns:
    if df[column].dtype == object:
        df[column] = df[column].map(normalize_text)

df["Count"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Count"], errors="coerce")
df["year"] = df["Date"].str[-2:].map(lambda value: 2000 + int(value) if str(value).isdigit() else None)

ranked = (
    df.groupby(["Facility Name", "Setting", "year"], dropna=False)["Count"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)

answer = sorted(
    ranked.loc[
        ranked["Setting"].eq("Ambulatory Surgery")
        & ranked["year"].eq(2019)
        & ranked["Count"].ge(35000),
        "Facility Name"
    ].tolist()
)
2.subquestion: Which hospitals had at least 35,000 ambulatory surgery encounters in 2019?
answer: UC San Diego Health Hillcrest - Hillcrest Medical Center

3.fact:
import io
import re
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/hospital-encounters-for-homeless-patients-79a6f/files/2019-2020-homeless-hospital-encounters-age-race-sex-expected-payer-by-facility.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"


def normalize_text(value):
    value = str(value).replace("\n", " ").replace("\x96", "-").replace("\x97", "-").replace("–", "-").replace("—", "-").strip()
    value = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", value)
    return "" if value.lower() == "nan" else value

obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)
df.columns = [normalize_text(column) for column in df.columns]
for column in df.columns:
    if df[column].dtype == object:
        df[column] = df[column].map(normalize_text)

df["Encounters"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Encounters"], errors="coerce")

county_totals = (
    df[
        df["FacilityName"].eq("Stanford Health Care")
        & df["EncounterType"].eq("Inpatient Hospitalizations")
        & df["HomelessIndicator"].eq("Homeless")
        & df["Demographic"].eq("Age")
    ]
    .groupby("HospitalCounty")["Encounters"]
    .sum()
    .sort_values(ascending=False)
)

answer = county_totals.index[0] + " County"
3.subquestion: For the hospital from <hop 1 answer>, which county had the highest total of homeless inpatient encounters broken down by age?

4.fact: Home to Silicon Valley, Santa Clara County is an economic center for high technology, and had the third-highest gross domestic product (GDP) per capita in the world as of 2015 behind Zürich and Oslo, according to the Brookings Institution.Silicon Valley Business Journal – San Jose Area has World's Third-Highest GDP Per Capita, Brookings Says
4.subquestion: As of 2015, what was <hop 2 answer>'s world rank for gross domestic product per capita?

reasoning_hops.title: Identify The Hospital
reasoning: Node 1: In the 2022 TAVR mortality rows, keep hospitals with `Total Hospital TAVR Cases >= 250` and `Performance Rating = Average`.
Node 2: In the utilization trends table, sum `Count` by `Facility Name`, `Setting`, and parsed year, then keep hospitals with `Setting = Ambulatory Surgery`, `year = 2019`, and `Count >= 35000`.
Necessity check: omitting node 1 leaves 3 hospitals: `Stanford Health Care`, `UC San Diego Health Hillcrest - Hillcrest Medical Center`, and `UCSF Medical Center`.

Necessity check: omitting node 2 leaves 5 hospitals: `Cedars-Sinai Medical Center`, `Kaiser Foundation Hospital - San Francisco`, `Mercy General Hospital`, `Scripps Memorial Hospital - La Jolla`, and `Stanford Health Care`.
Hop result: intersect the two analytical candidate sets from nodes 1 and 2. The only hospital that satisfies both conditions is `Stanford Health Care`.

reasoning_hops.title: Rank The County
reasoning: Node 3: In the homeless-hospital-encounter table, filter to `FacilityName = <hop 1 answer>`, `EncounterType = Inpatient Hospitalizations`, `HomelessIndicator = Homeless`, and `Demographic = Age`, then sum `Encounters` by `HospitalCounty` and rank descending.
Hop result: `Santa Clara County` ranks first with `1131` homeless inpatient age-sliced encounters.

reasoning_hops.title: Find The GDP-Per-Capita Rank
reasoning: Node 4 uses the county from `<hop 2 answer>` and retrieves its 2015 world rank for gross domestic product per capita.
